messages.text: When the M6 ​​belt was found to be out of position, maintenance was called.
Good morning. Maintenance is currently working on the guillotine.
Chris G, Chris J, and Neitan R are working to resolve the guillotine issue; all machines are in night mode.
The guillotine is already up and running, ready to start.

Line is ready to run. The working theory from Sollich is that the doors on the guillotine can trigger a safety alarm if they are shut "wrong" and then all of the power cycling to try and clear the alarm corrupted the program on the Rexroth PLC. The program has been corrected. The fix is to use the reset alarm button within the guillotine HMI instead of cycling power. And also if we cycle power make sure the entire system has rebooted before turning power off again.
The programmer is in the US for the next 3 weeks so let me know if there are additional problems and I can get him to respond during 1st shift hours.
I just put the Enrobers in temper

Thank you for the follow up Matt. 

Rich Brierley, Frederick Peairs, Grey Boscan

Let’s work on an OPL that is done today so we can train all the ops and then Preston Elmer can use this to train his technicians.
I will take care of this OPL today. I've got all the pictures
The temper is good
I can't get the M6 belt to align without adjustments on this broken nose piece. This piece is going to have to be removed for repair.
Maintenance has it out of service for repairs.
We are currently waiting for temper, and Maintenance is also working on the M6. Once we have temper and the M6 is ready, we will start the line.

Spoke with Nathan hes is working on fixing the belt it is estimated to take 1-2 hours to get the belt fixed. I have sent 16 EE's home. I kept 1 Cavanna operator and the Front half of the TL. If the belt is fixed before 2nd shift we will start the line

Dakota Adair Jody Howse Justin Garner, 1st shift has been using the quick view checklist for Jar Area AND MMs, instead of Jar Area OR MMs. Is that the correct list to follow? Or do we need to adjust course, so to speak?
These are the areas that we are doing today on 1st shift. We are currently doing LOTO's and disassembling it right now
We are starting to clean the areas listed in Section 2 of the Jar Area & MM Quick View.

We need to do the jar and MM’s the only issue is we won’t be able to clean the jar area until 2am tonight so anything that is on our section that has jars place those EE’s on another portion of the line
We can do the jar filling conveyor on our shift though

We are behind on the Enrober because we have been waiting for QA to inspect it. The lead called QA at 2pm and they were not available to inpect it. We are just barely inspecting the Enrober at 3:47pm. Charity Young Miriah Manzanares can we get more support on this please? We have to complete our section before going home, so if we can not get the support on time, we are forced to keep EE's here until everything is complete.

Katya
Thank you for taking the time to discuss this with me in person.
Moving forward, please encourage your team to connect with the shift supervisor regarding quality-related timing concerns, including call acknowledgments and discussions around expected arrival times. The supervisor is responsible for helping prioritize activities as needed. If support is not received at that level, please escalate those situations to Charity Young
Mary Angelica Ebrima — please continue to partner closely with Operations by providing clear visibility into which team members are supporting each production line, along with any planned projects or additional work assignments. This will help ensure informed decision-making and improve communication around releases and priorities.

The QA assigned to the line was occupied inspecting the Top Matrix units, which were behind schedule on cleaning activities. This made it difficult to perform continuous visual inspections on the line. The area supervisor was aware of my location and responsibilities throughout the process. QA remained on the production line for the entire shift the problem was I was needed in more then one area.  I will make sure I will notify.
Sometimes it is necessary to be in two areas at the same time. If you speak with the supervisor, she can confirm and provide additional information regarding the situation.
Nathan Topham, Mary Montilla, Charity Young, Miriah Manzanares

Mariela, I appreciate you sharing this information with me.
Tomorrow, I’ll follow up with you and the production team to better understand how those two situations could have been prioritized differently. The goal is to help you and the rest of the team plan your time more effectively and avoid feeling rushed or pressured.
Thank you Miriah M. That’s very considerate of you

No. This is an all employee requirement. All observations of pests need to be logged.

Here the plan for the rest of the day.
We are changing back and forth to Bequet twice to confirm the grippers can run both types of TW film without issues and to give more practice to the operators.
21184 is the WIP single piece item number for BET
Changeover from BEQ to wax was successful

CHANGEOVER STEPS to Wax paper
1.-  Move rolls position to the checkmark, distance should be 94mm from the wall of the machine to the front of the plexiglass plate.


2. Loosen the campling joint. Red dot up is loose position, red dot down is close position.


3. With a 10mm spacer adjust the screws for the travel distance of the twister grippers



4. Adjust the distance between drums, for wax paper should be 36.5cm



5. Move campling joint to the close position. Red dot down.

Curt Willis Chris Givens Jorge Carrillo

We still haven’t been able to effectively hold temper since the work. 

Robert Villarreal,

Please spend your morning on ML1.

Good morning Imperial,
Will you please put crystallization down to 81.0
I want to go to 80.5 but we'll do it at 0.5 adjustments per hour
Still, I don't believe all our circ pumps are working correctly, namely the preheat one.. which is why i want to reheat to destroy crystals
Robert Villarreal do you have old settings from 12g plain that had jacket temps in the 80s?
I will have to look, we used to run the jacket at 87 for plain and 89 on crisp
In reheat the piping is raising with the PV very well. It used to feel much colder for a long time
Do you think we can start lowering it back to normal? That would definitely affect temper if we're overheating the pipes now

For months I have never felt temperatures in the waterjacket like this. It has never felt like 96. It has never felt like 89 or 87. It's always been maybe 95 at one spot, 85 at another spot. But I am feeling even temps across the water jacket from outlet to return and it feels like it reacts to set point which is something I have not seen. I am going to setpoints from months ago after this reheat I just started.
Yes
Are these setpoints captured on a CL? It makes sense that if we've been having issues keeping a water jacket heat up, that our temps were pushed higher to compensate.
Decent result, taking another to see if it repeats

21184 is the WIP single piece item number for BET
Changeover from BEQ to wax was successful

CHANGEOVER STEPS to Wax paper
1.-  Move rolls position to the checkmark, distance should be 94mm from the wall of the machine to the front of the plexiglass plate.


2. Loosen the campling joint. Red dot up is loose position, red dot down is close position.


3. With a 10mm spacer adjust the screws for the travel distance of the twister grippers



4. Adjust the distance between drums, for wax paper should be 36.5cm



5. Move campling joint to the close position. Red dot down.

Curt Willis Chris Givens Jorge Carrillo
